summary:
During tooth formation, vitamin A primarily affects dental structures related to epithelial origins; thus, enamel may suffer the greatest impact among these options as an external covering developed directly from such layers. Enamel appears particularly sensitive because it forms early through highly regulated processes involving extensive cellular proliferation typical of rapidly growing epithelia requiring robust support mechanisms provided by adequate nutrition like vitamins essential for structural integrities. \boxed{Enamel}
